spec_match.rationale: The demo places <selectedcontent> mirrors outside of the <select> element without any linking mechanism, which completely contradicts the customizable select specification where <selectedcontent> must be a descendant of <select>.

interactive.rationale: Allows selection change, but because there is no script block to simulate the update, the external <selectedcontent> tags remain completely blank and will never update in any stable browser.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The text blurb correctly details that all mirrors should update simultaneously, but the code implementation fails to follow the spec rules.

edge_cases.rationale: It fails to implement working fallback or correct markup, resulting in a broken concept page in both supported and unsupported browsers.

references.rationale: Includes links back to the feature index.

openQuestions.title: Fix HTML structure to place mirrors inside <select>
openQuestions.detail: The mirrors must be descendants of the <select> element in order to be natively cloned by the browser. Placing them outside <select> breaks the spec. Alternatively, if an external linking mechanism like a `for` attribute is supported by a specific draft, it should be documented and declared, but the current HTML lists them as raw unlinked <selectedcontent> elements.

summary: A fundamentally broken implementation of customizable select mirrors, placing <selectedcontent> tags completely outside the parent <select> where they can never receive native updates.
